service_line_config.name: ai_ml
service_line_config.tech_context: This project involves AI/ML model development and deployment. Key considerations: - ML Frameworks: scikit-learn, TensorFlow, PyTorch, XGBoost, LightGBM - Data Processing: pandas, NumPy, PySpark, Dask for large-scale data - Feature Engineering: feature stores (Feast, Tecton), feature pipelines - Model Training: hyperparameter tuning (Optuna, Ray Tune), experiment tracking (MLflow, W&B) - Model Serving: FastAPI, TensorFlow Serving, TorchServe, Triton Inference Server - MLOps: MLflow, Kubeflow, SageMaker, Vertex AI, Azure ML - Data Versioning: DVC, LakeFS, Delta Lake - Monitoring: model drift detection, data quality monitoring, prediction logging - Infrastructure: GPU compute, distributed training, model registries

artifact_overrides.module_extraction: Map modules to ML pipeline stages: data ingestion, feature engineering, model training, model serving, monitoring. Also consider separate modules for data quality, experimentation, and MLOps infrastructure.
artifact_overrides.functional_req: - Specify the ML task type: classification, regression, NLP, computer vision, recommendation - Define input data sources, formats, and volume - Specify model performance requirements (accuracy, F1, latency) - Define training pipeline: data prep, feature engineering, training, evaluation, registration - Define serving requirements: batch vs real-time, API contract, response format
artifact_overrides.nonfunctional_req: - Model inference latency: p50, p95, p99 targets - Training pipeline execution time and compute budget - Model accuracy thresholds for production deployment (precision, recall, F1, AUC) - Data freshness: maximum age of training data, retraining frequency - Model drift detection: statistical thresholds for triggering retraining - Data quality: completeness, consistency, and validity thresholds - GPU/compute cost budgets for training and inference
artifact_overrides.task: - Data pipeline: ingestion, cleaning, validation, transformation - Feature engineering: feature computation, feature store integration - Model development: algorithm selection, training scripts, hyperparameter tuning - Evaluation: metric computation, model comparison, bias/fairness analysis - Model registry: versioning, metadata, approval workflow - Serving: API development, model loading, batch prediction pipeline - Monitoring: drift detection, prediction logging, alerting - MLOps: CI/CD for ML, automated retraining triggers, A/B testing infrastructure
artifact_overrides.test_case: - Test data pipeline with missing, malformed, and edge-case data - Test feature computation correctness with known input/output pairs - Test model inference with representative and adversarial inputs - Test serving API latency under concurrent load - Test model versioning and rollback procedures - Validate model fairness across protected demographic groups - Test drift detection alerts with synthetic distribution shifts
artifact_overrides.architecture: - Data architecture: data lake/warehouse, ingestion pipelines, data versioning - Feature architecture: feature store, computation pipelines, online/offline serving - Training architecture: experiment tracking, distributed training, hyperparameter search - Model registry: versioning, metadata, promotion workflow - Serving architecture: model serving infrastructure, scaling, A/B routing - Monitoring architecture: prediction logging, drift detection, alerting - MLOps: CI/CD pipelines, automated retraining, model governance
artifact_overrides.risk_entry: - Model accuracy degradation from data drift or concept drift - Training data quality issues leading to biased or incorrect models - GPU compute costs exceeding budget during hyperparameter search - Model serving latency spikes under unexpected traffic patterns - Lack of model interpretability for regulated use cases - Data privacy violations in training data handling - Model versioning confusion causing wrong model in production
